input.question: We are a student engineering team designing a vehicle for the FSAE competition. Attached is the FSAE rules document. Also attached is an image that shows an engineering drawing of the rear wheel package on the top accompanied by six CAD views of the rear wheel package on the bottom. The six CAD views each feature a different orientation of our design, so that 3D information about our design can be inferred. In the CAD views, the fastner highlighted in pink is the critical fastener. The CAD views are provided to contextualize the engineering drawing, which has the same orientation as one of the six CAD views. All units displayed in the engineering drawing have units of mm. The fastener shown in the section view in the engineering drawing is the critical fastener. It has 1 thread/mm. Based on the engineering drawing, does our design comply with rule T.8.2.4 specified in the FSAE rule document? Only use dimensions explicitly shown in the engineering drawing to answer the question. If a dimension is not explicitly shown, you can assume that it complies with the rules. First provide an explanation for your answer (begin it with 'Explanation:'). Then provide just a yes/no answer (begin it with 'Answer:') that summarizes your response.

retrieved_context:
RULE: t.8.2.4
            TITLE: a minimum of two full threads must project from any lock nut.

            CONTENT:
            rule T.8.2.4: A minimum of two full threads must project from any lock nut.
T.8.2.4 A minimum of two full threads must project from any lock nut.

intermediate_steps.founded_objects: dimension arrow, dimension line, thread representation

final_answer:
Explanation: Rule T.8.2.4 requires that a minimum of two full threads must project from any lock nut. The engineering drawing shows the fastener (lock nut) in Detail A, but none of the explicitly provided dimensions (such as .9, 0.47, or 0.41) quantify or confirm the minimum required thread projection length of two full threads. Since the dimension necessary to verify compliance with this critical rule is not explicitly shown, compliance cannot be confirmed based solely on the provided drawing dimensions.

Answer: No
